concepts.label: Percentage-based coordinate system
concepts.statement: Coordinates are stored as % of page dimensions (0–100) for scale-independence. Conversion to PDF user units (points) happens at annotation time by multiplying percentage by page dimension and dividing by 100.

concepts.label: Y-coordinate flip from top to bottom-left
concepts.rule: yPct is measured from top of page (0 = top); PDF Y origin is bottom-left. Conversion formula: `y = pageH - (yPct / 100) * pageH - height` to account for both origin shift and element height.

concepts.label: Disable object streams on PDF save
concepts.statement: useObjectStreams: false must be set on all PDF saves to prevent font regression.

concepts.label: Sticky note fixed proportional dimensions
concepts.rule: Sticky notes use fixed size: 15% of page width and 9% of page height, independent of text length.

concepts.label: Checkmark drawn as two-stroke V shape
concepts.rule: Checkmark (✓) rendered with two lines: (1) short down-left stroke from (cx, cy + sz*0.35) to (cx + sz*0.38, cy); (2) long up-right stroke from (cx + sz*0.38, cy) to (cx + sz, cy + sz*0.75). Both strokes use thickness 2.2.

concepts.label: Font embedding only when needed
concepts.match_rule: Helvetica font is embedded once and only if at least one 'sticky-note' annotation exists in the batch; otherwise stickyFont is null.

concepts.label: Sticky note text truncation limit
concepts.statement: Sticky note text is truncated to 120 characters maximum before rendering.

concepts.label: Canvas-to-PDF coordinate transform
concepts.statement: Canvas uses top-left origin; PDF uses bottom-left origin. For a block at canvas (top, left, width, height) on a page of height pageH, the PDF equivalent is: coverX = left / viewportScale, coverY = pageH - (top + height) / viewportScale, coverW = width / viewportScale, coverH = height / viewportScale.

concepts.label: Anchor item PDF space coordinates
concepts.statement: The anchor item's pdfX and pdfY are already in PDF coordinate space and require no reverse-computation; they are used directly as the draw origin for text placement.

concepts.label: White rectangle mask for text replacement
concepts.statement: Before drawing replacement text, apply a white opaque rectangle (1 unit oversized on all sides) at the original block bounds to mask/cover the underlying PDF content.

concepts.label: Font embedding deduplication
concepts.statement: Deduplicate font embeddings across all canvas edits by caching font objects by name, avoiding redundant embedFont calls for the same font used in multiple textboxes.

concepts.label: PDF save without object streams
concepts.statement: All PDF saves must use `useObjectStreams: false` to ensure compatibility and predictable output structure.

concepts.label: Client-side PDF compression scope and limitations
concepts.statement: PDF compression removes redundant cross-reference data and re-serialises the PDF (10–30% reduction on unoptimised files), but does NOT recompress embedded images (requires server-side tooling). UI must be honest about this limitation.

concepts.label: PDF save uses useObjectStreams: true
concepts.statement: All PDF saves must use `useObjectStreams: true` to enable object stream compression for size reduction.

concepts.label: PDF metadata update disabled during load
concepts.statement: PDF documents are loaded with `updateMetadata: false` to preserve original metadata and avoid unintended side effects.

concepts.label: Three coordinate systems (PDF space, Canvas space, Percentage space)
concepts.statement: PDF space: origin BOTTOM-LEFT, Y increases upward, units in PDF user units (1 pt = 1/72 inch), used for pdf-lib calls. Canvas/CSS space: origin TOP-LEFT, Y increases downward, units in CSS pixels scaled by zoom, used for overlay positioning. Percentage space: 0–100 representing page dimension fractions, scale-independent, used by SignTool UI.

concepts.label: Y-axis conversion formula accounting for element height and page origin
concepts.statement: All Y-axis conversions between Canvas (top-left origin, Y↓) and PDF (bottom-left origin, Y↑) must apply: canvasY = (pageHeightPdf - pdfY - elementHeightPdf) * scale. When converting a bare point (not a sized element), elementHeightCanvas must be 0. pdf-lib's drawImage anchors at bottom-left, requiring this adjustment.

concepts.label: Percentage UI coords represent visual centre; convert to pdf-lib bottom-left anchor
concepts.statement: SignTool UI stores xPct/yPct as centre coordinates (rendered with transform: translate(-50%, -50%)). pdf-lib drawImage() anchors at bottom-left. Conversion must: (1) compute centre in PDF units, (2) shift by half dimensions to get bottom-left corner: x = centrePdfX - widthPdf/2; y = pageHeightPdf - centrePdfY_fromTop - heightPdf/2.

concepts.label: Metric-compatible font substitution via CSS variables
concepts.statement: Arimo/Tinos/Cousine are loaded via next/font CSS vars and are metric-compatible with Arial/Times/Courier respectively, enabling safe visual substitution in the editor overlay while preserving PDF structure.

concepts.label: Bold/italic not preserved in PDF output
concepts.statement: Bold/italic styling is shown in the editor overlay but not persisted when saving the PDF. Tracked in docs/lessons-learned.md.

concepts.label: Heavily subsetted custom fonts fall back to Arial
concepts.statement: Custom corporate fonts that are heavily subsetted cannot be reliably mapped and fall back to Arial. Tracked in docs/lessons-learned.md.

concepts.label: Helvetica/Courier use Oblique; Times uses Italic
concepts.statement: When applying italic styling to PDF-lib standard fonts: Helvetica and Courier families use the 'Oblique' suffix, while Times family uses 'Italic' suffix.

concepts.label: Font match confidence level
concepts.statement: A FontMatch has confidence 'high' when matched against WEB_FONT_RULES, or confidence 'fallback' when no rule matched and Arial/Helvetica is used as default.

concepts.label: All PageGrid coordinates are in canvas space (CSS pixels, top-left origin)
concepts.statement: All coordinates returned by extractPageGrid and used by snapToGrid are in CANVAS space (CSS pixels, top-left origin) without further conversion needed by the UI.

concepts.label: Baselines use glyph top edge instead of calculated baseline position
concepts.statement: For baseline snapping, use the raw canvas Y (top edge of the glyph box) rather than computing the true baseline ~80% below, because clustering handles variation sufficiently and avoids requiring per-glyph descent data.

concepts.label: column-start
concepts.statement: A text item is a column-start if there is no other item whose right edge falls within COLUMN_GAP_PX pixels to the item's left; these left-edge X values become column markers after deduplication.

concepts.label: AcroForm field detection not yet implemented
concepts.statement: AcroForm field detection is intentionally left for a later sprint — formFields is always an empty array for now (see task spec note).

concepts.label: Sub-pixel baseline clustering tolerance
concepts.rule: BASELINE_CLUSTER_PX = 4px; values within 4px are merged into the same baseline to tolerate sub-pixel rendering differences between adjacent words on a line.

concepts.label: Column left-edge clustering tolerance
concepts.rule: COLUMN_CLUSTER_PX = 6px; column left edges within 6px are deduplicated into a single column marker.

concepts.label: Inline flow gap threshold for column detection
concepts.rule: COLUMN_GAP_PX = 20px; items are considered part of the same inline flow (and not independent column starts) if their right edge is within 20px of the next item's left edge.

concepts.label: Default snap threshold is 12px
concepts.statement: The default threshold for snapToGrid is 12 CSS pixels; this defines the maximum distance to snap a user placement to the nearest grid line.

concepts.label: Disable object streams on PDF save
concepts.statement: All PDF saves must use useObjectStreams: false to ensure compatibility

concepts.label: Base64 data URL decoding pattern
concepts.match_rule: data:[mime/type];base64,[BASE64_STRING] split on comma, take [1], decode with atomb(), convert to Uint8Array via charCodeAt

concepts.label: Image embedding conditional on MIME type
Disable useObjectStreams on pdf save
concepts.statement: All pdfDoc.save() invocations across the project must use useObjectStreams: false. Compressed object streams cause some PDF viewers to fall back to a default sans-serif for unedited text items, producing visible rendering regressions.

concepts.label: Disable object streams on all PDF saves
concepts.statement: useObjectStreams: false must be set on all pdfDoc.save() calls to ensure compatibility with downstream PDF processing

concepts.label: afterPageIndex parameter semantics
concepts.statement: afterPageIndex is 0-based and the insertion point is afterPageIndex + 1; passing afterPageIndex = pageCount - 1 appends at the end

concepts.label: PDF redaction via content stream surgery with decompression
concepts.statement: Redaction must decompress FlateDecode streams, blank matching text operators (Tj/TJ/'" ), and write back as uncompressed streams. This removes text from the PDF data layer, not just visually.

concepts.label: PDF save must disable object streams
concepts.statement: All PDF saves must use useObjectStreams: false to ensure compatibility and predictable stream handling.

concepts.label: Matched text blanked with space characters
concepts.rule: When redacting matched text in Tj/TJ operators, replace each non-space character with a space (via `/[^ ]/g` substitution) to preserve glyph-advance metrics.

concepts.label: PDF text operators for redaction matching
concepts.match_rule: Regex patterns matching PDF text show operators: Tj (literal string), TJ (array of strings/numbers), ' (single-quote shorthand), " (double-quote shorthand). Patterns: `\(([^\\)]*(?:\\.[^\\)]*)*)\)\s*(Tj|'|")` for literal strings; `\[([^\]]*)\]\s*TJ` for arrays.

concepts.label: Latin-1 encoding for PDF content stream text
concepts.statement: PDF content streams are decoded/encoded using latin-1 (ISO-8859-1), not UTF-8. TextDecoder('latin1') for decompressed bytes; custom strToBytes function for re-encoding to preserve byte-for-byte character mapping.

concepts.label: Target text matching is case-insensitive
concepts.statement: Text redaction searches use toLowerCase() on both target and content to perform case-insensitive matching, ensuring 'Hello', 'hello', 'HELLO' are all found and blanked.

concepts.label: Skip streams with unsupported filters or decompression failures
concepts.statement: Streams with unsupported compression filters (JBIG2, CCITTFax, etc.) or failed decompression are silently skipped; no redaction is performed on such streams.

concepts.label: Redacted streams written back uncompressed
concepts.statement: After redaction, modified content streams are written back without compression. Filter and DecodeParms dictionary entries are deleted, and Length is set to the new uncompressed byte count.

concepts.label: Mask rectangle descender and ascender proportional to font size
concepts.rule: descenderDepth = max(2, fs × 0.25); ascenderHeight = fs × 0.85; mask height = ascenderHeight + descenderDepth + 2

concepts.label: Disable useObjectStreams on all PDF saves
concepts.statement: pdfDoc.save({ useObjectStreams: false }) — prevents pdf-lib from repacking font resource cross-references into compressed object streams, which causes some PDF viewers to fall back to a default sans-serif for unedited text items.

concepts.label: Font cache deduplication strategy
concepts.statement: One embedded font per unique standard font name; fontCache is a Map keyed by standardFontName to avoid re-embedding identical fonts across multiple edits.

concepts.label: Bold/italic override precedence
concepts.statement: effectiveBold = fieldData.bold ?? fontMatch.bold; effectiveItalic = fieldData.italic ?? fontMatch.italic. User toolbar overrides take precedence over auto-detected values from original font name.

concepts.label: Soft guard on replacement text width
concepts.statement: maxWidth: originalWidth × 3 — generous but bounded; longer replacements will overflow but won't corrupt other text.

concepts.label: Disable useObjectStreams on PDF saves to prevent font corruption
concepts.statement: All PDFDocument.save() calls must use { useObjectStreams: false } to prevent font cross-reference corruption in PDF viewers that misparse PDF 1.5 object streams.

concepts.label: Percentage-based placement coordinates
concepts.statement: Placement coordinates from the UI are percentages of page dimensions (0–100). This makes them scale-independent and independent of zoom factor. The single source of truth for converting percentage coordinates to PDF user-unit coordinates is pctToPdfCoords in coords.ts.

concepts.label: PDF user-unit coordinate system
concepts.statement: pdf-lib drawImage() expects PDF user-unit coordinates with bottom-left origin. Placement coordinates (percentage-based, centre-origin) must be converted via pctToPdfCoords.

concepts.label: 1-indexed page numbering from UI
concepts.match_rule: pageNum must be decremented by 1 before array access; error messages must reference the 1-indexed value.

concepts.label: Typed signature default width is 30% of page width
concepts.statement: When widthPct is not specified in TypedSignatureEntry, default to 30% of page width for font sizing calculations.

concepts.label: Typed signature font size range
concepts.statement: Font size for typed signatures starts at 48pt and shrinks by 2pt increments until text fits target width, with a floor of 8pt.

concepts.label: Data URL decoding supports browser and Node.js
concepts.statement: dataUrlToUint8Array must handle both browser (atob) and Node.js (Buffer) environments to support test runners.

concepts.label: Always disable useObjectStreams on PDF saves
concepts.statement: All PDFDocument.save() calls must use { useObjectStreams: false } to ensure broad compatibility and consistent output formatting across split operations.

concepts.label: Page range indexing convention
concepts.statement: All user-facing page indices in range { start, end } are 1-indexed and inclusive on both ends. Internally converted to 0-indexed arrays for copyPages().

concepts.label: PDFDocument.create() + copyPages() consistency
concepts.rule: Use PDFDocument.create() followed by copyPages() + addPage() loop pattern for all PDF transformations to maintain consistency with merge.ts utility.

concepts.label: PDF coordinate space
concepts.statement: Bottom-left origin coordinate system used by pdf-lib for save operations. Contrast with canvas coordinate space (top-left origin) used for overlay positioning.

concepts.label: Canvas coordinate space
concepts.statement: Top-left origin coordinate system used for overlay positioning and Fabric canvas rendering. Contrast with PDF coordinate space (bottom-left origin).

concepts.label: Text item ID stable key format
Annotation coordinates as page percentage
concepts.rule: All annotation types (TextHighlight, StickyNote, CheckAnnotation) use xPct/yPct normalized to 0–100 as % of page dimensions, with origin at top-left.

concepts.label: Anchor item
concepts.statement: An ExtractedTextItem whose pdfX/pdfY coordinates are used as the draw origin for a replacement or canvas text block placement. The reference point for text positioning in PDF space.

concepts.label: Fabric textbox fontSize canvas-to-PDF conversion
concepts.rule: fontSize stored in canvas pixels; must be divided by scale factor to convert to PDF points for save.

concepts.label: Committed edits persist across tool switch
concepts.statement: CommittedEdit mirrors FabricTextboxExport structure to enable use as download fallback when CanvasTextLayer is unmounted (edit tool becomes inactive). Allows edit recovery without active layer ref.

concepts.label: Image placement uses CENTER point
concepts.rule: ImageEntry xPct/yPct specify the CENTER of the image bounding box (0–100 as % of page), not top-left.

concepts.label: Signature entry text/drawing modal union
concepts.rule: SigEntry has optional text and optional drawingDataUrl; exactly one should be populated at render time (typed vs. hand-drawn signature).
